# Terrarium

> Terrarium is a free, open-source macOS application that instantly previews React components (.tsx and .jsx files) generated by Claude AI or written by hand. It requires zero configuration, automatically detects and installs npm dependencies, supports Tailwind CSS v3 out of the box, and live-reloads on every file save. Terrarium is built for developers who want to iterate on React UIs without setting up a full project environment.

## Key Facts

- Author: Michelle Mayes
- License: MIT
- Platform: macOS 12 (Monterey) or later
- Runtime requirement: Node.js 18 or later
- Install command: `brew tap michellemayes/terrarium && brew install --cask terrarium`
- Source code: https://github.com/michellemayes/terrarium
- Releases: https://github.com/michellemayes/terrarium/releases/latest
- Current version: 1.0.0

## What Terrarium Does

Terrarium renders React components directly from .tsx and .jsx files with zero configuration. Open a file — via drag-and-drop, file picker, double-click, or the `terrarium file.tsx` CLI — and Terrarium bundles it with esbuild, auto-installs any npm packages the file imports, and displays the rendered result. Every file save triggers an instant re-bundle and re-render, so editing in any external editor (VS Code, Zed, Cursor, Claude Code) keeps the preview live.

## Use Cases

- Preview Claude AI React artifacts outside the chat window
- Live-reload React components during Claude Code pair-programming sessions
- Test .tsx or .jsx files without creating a package.json or build configuration
- Prototype Tailwind CSS v3 UIs with zero setup overhead
- Demonstrate isolated components without booting a full dev server

## Features

- Zero-config dependency installation via auto-detected npm imports
- File watcher with instant reload on save
- Built-in Tailwind CSS v3 with build-time JIT generation
- Collapsible error overlay with line numbers that preserves the last good render
- Multiple open methods: drag-and-drop, CLI, file picker, dock, and double-click
- Claude Code integration: run `terrarium` in one terminal, Claude Code in another, and every save live-reloads the preview
- JSX support alongside TSX (as of v0.3.0)
- Pre-installed common dependencies for instant first render

## Security

Terrarium executes the code inside .tsx and .jsx files in order to render them. Only open files you trust — treat .tsx and .jsx files like executables. Terrarium is open source under the MIT License, and all source code is available for audit at https://github.com/michellemayes/terrarium.

## Links
